name: Dan Jurafsky
sub_directions: Social and Ethical Implications of NLP/LLMs
Computational Linguistics and Pragmatics for LLM Behavior
NLP applications in Food Science and Sustainability
Robust Speech Recognition for Language Disparities
recent_papers: Accommodation and Epistemic Vigilance: A Pragmatic Account of Why LLMs Fail to Challenge Harmful Beliefs
Sycophantic AI Decreases Prosocial Intentions and Promotes Dependence.
interest_paragraph: I am particularly drawn to Professor Jurafsky's interdisciplinary approach, especially his work on the social and ethical implications of NLP, as seen in papers like 'Sycophantic AI Decreases Prosocial Intentions and Promotes Dependence.' The application of computational linguistics to understand LLM behavior and enhance creativity, alongside the novel direction of NLP for Food Science and sustainability, deeply resonates with my research interests. I am eager to explore how advanced LLMs can be developed to be more robust and socially aware, leveraging insights from pragmatics and cognitive science.
